settings.editor:
Knowledge Transfer Director/Lead Instructor

Mission:

The mission of the Knowledge Transfer Director/Lead Instructor is to act as a subject matter expert focused on helping AEC organizations develop more effective people leaders and project managers. The knowledge transfer director will do this by helping design and deliver (instructor) custom learning and development programs and delivering content (i.e. articles, podcasts, videos, etc.).

Outcomes:

1. Service clients by serving as a subject matter expert through EMI's program design process including:

a. Interview program participants, (leadership and project managers) prior to the program design,

b. Review and input on the program curriculum during development, and

c. Lead design meetings with the client.

2. Serve as the lead instructor for EMI's custom leadership and project management development programs including:

a. Give 2 to 4 virtual sessions per week focused on participant engagement.

b. Help to guide and grow other EMI instructors through training and mentoring. 

c. Strive to engage participants and seek 100% assignment completion.

3. Prepare and deliver content related to people leadership and project management in the AEC industry including written articles on EMI's blog or guest posts for other organizations, podcast interviews for EMI, or appearing as a guest on other shows. Videos, including videos for any of EMI's three YouTube channels or videos related to EMI's products, for example, PM AssistTM - a new service focused on reinforcing EMI's PM development programs.

Qualifications

  • A bachelor's degree from an accredited college or university in engineering, architecture, land surveying or related field.
  • Minimum of five (5) years of relevant experience in your technical field as a project manager.
  • Engineering, PMP or equivalent license/certification highly recommended, but not required.
  • Speaking/instruction skills: previous experience speaking in public or presenting is a MUST.
  • Toastmasters experience/credentials (i.e. Competent Communicator) a plus.

Benefits

  • Work Location: Remote with 3 to 5 trips per year to Headquarters (Midland Park, NJ) plus travel to conferences if you are interested.
  • Full -Time Hours: The position is full-time and requires 40 hours per week, but EMI is very flexible as to the time of day your work is conducted.
  • Other benefits include health, dental, vision, 401k, profit sharing, and more

Job Type

Full-Time

Expected Salary

$80,000 to $115,000*

*Salary range may vary based on applicants experience level, and could be higher than indicated, especially with license/certification.

Work Location

Remote with 3 to 5 trips per year to NJ Headquarters

What Makes the Engineering Management Institute a Great Place to Work…

The Engineering Management Institute is the gold standard learning and development resource for engineers and technical professionals worldwide. EMI empowers technical professionals to become the leaders they need to be to solve the world's biggest problems by providing highly relevant content and training that transfers back to their jobs.

EMI is a great place to work as we work with top engineering organizations globally, we are growing fast, and focus greatly on helping our team members achieve their personal and professional goals. We do this by following our guiding principles (give, guide, and grow).  We ask all EMI team members to give their all, guide each other, and grow together.  We also employ a quarterly profit sharing program, so that as EMI grows, all team members are rewarded monetarily for their contributions to EMI's growth.

If you are interested in joining a dynamic team, and working on exciting growth-oriented projects, please consider this role. Joining the EMI team may be an avenue to fast-track the achievement of your professional and personal goals.

Apply Today by Completing this Form
